Both web searches returned no useful results, and the snippet search surfaced only tangential kinetics papers. I'll note that and proceed from established domain knowledge, which is solid ground here.

**What I can extract from the literature that is well-established** (Knowles, Cohen, Michaels, Linse work on Aβ42 kinetics — reflected in the seed program naming and the master-equation framework): amyloid fibril mass follows an integrated rate law where aggregate growth is driven by (1) primary nucleation from free monomer, (2) elongation of existing fibrils, and (3) monomer-dependent **secondary nucleation** — a positive feedback where existing aggregate surface catalyzes new nuclei, i.e. a `monomer × aggregate` autocatalytic term.
